file_name: PlayboyEnterprisesInc_20090220_10-QA_EX-10.2_4091580_EX-10.2_Content License Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Sales-Purchase Agreement1.txt

perturbation.original_text: 10.1. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated as provided under this Agreement, shall terminate five (5) years following the date of launch of the first Website, but in no event later than February 28, 2013 (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew for an additional period of three (3) years (such renewal and each subsequent renewal shall be defined as a "Renewal Term") provided that EFS achieves Net Merchandise Sales of at least ***** during the fourth year following launch of the first Website. In such event, the annual Minimum Royalty during the Renewal Term, if any, shall be equal to the greater of: (a) ***** of the actual Royalty paid to Client in Year 5; and (b) *****. The Initial Term and any and all Renewal Terms shall be collectively referred to as the "Term."
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated as provided under this Agreement, shall terminate five (5) years following the date of launch of the first Website, but in no event later than February 28, 2013 (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew for an additional period of three (3) years (such renewal and each subsequent renewal shall be defined as a "Renewal Term") regardless of whether EFS achieves Net Merchandise Sales of at least ***** during the fourth year following launch of the first Website. In such event, the annual Minimum Royalty during the Renewal Term, if any, shall be equal to the greater of: (a) ***** of the actual Royalty paid to Client in Year 5; and (b) *****. The Initial Term and any and all Renewal Terms shall be collectively referred to as the "Term."
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 10.1 states that the agreement automatically renews for an additional three years *provided that* EFS achieves a certain level of Net Merchandise Sales. The modified version removes this condition, stating that the agreement automatically renews *regardless* of whether EFS meets the sales target. This contradicts the original condition for renewal, creating uncertainty as to whether the sales target is actually required for the renewal to occur. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1. Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated as provided under this Agreement, shall terminate five (5) years following the date of launch of the first Website, but in no event later than February 28, 2013 (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew for an additional period of three (3) years (such renewal and each subsequent renewal shall be defined as a "Renewal Term") regardless of whether EFS achieves Net Merchandise Sales of at least ***** during the fourth year following launch of the first Website. In such event, the annual Minimum Royalty during the Renewal Term, if any, shall be equal to the greater of: (a) ***** of the actual Royalty paid to Client in Year 5; and (b) *****. The Initial Term and any and all Renewal Terms shall be collectively referred to as the "Term."
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement automatically renews contingent upon EFS achieving a specific sales target. The modified text removes this condition, stating the agreement renews 'regardless' of the sales target. This directly contradicts the original requirement for renewal within Section 10.1 itself. Thus the contradicted text and the changed text are one and the same. This contradiction creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the renewal terms.

perturbation.original_text: EFS agrees to employ certain individuals who are currently employed by the Client (names, titles and current salaries are listed in Exhibit 6), the hiring of whom shall be complete no later than March 1, 2008. Client shall coordinate with EFS regarding the termination of these employees and their subsequent hire by EFS. Each of the hired employees shall be required to comply with all policies and procedures of EFS which are generally applicable to its employees; provided however that levels of seniority and tenure of hired employees (as they relate to vacation time, etc.) will carry over to EFS. Client shall have 5 Source: PLAYBOY ENTERPRISES INC, 10-Q/A, 2/20/2009 input and approval rights regarding the hiring of key positions in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business, including replacement personnel, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld. EFS agrees that ***** primary responsibilities will continue to be the Playboy Commerce Business.
perturbation.changed_text: EFS agrees to employ certain individuals who are currently employed by the Client (names, titles and current salaries are listed in Exhibit 6), the hiring of whom may or may not be complete no later than March 1, 2008, pending EFS's discresion. Client shall not coordinate with EFS regarding the termination of these employees and their subsequent hire by EFS, as EFS will take care of that directly. Each of the hired employees shall be required to comply with all policies and procedures of EFS which are generally applicable to its employees; provided however that levels of seniority and tenure of hired employees (as they relate to vacation time, etc.) may or may not carry over to EFS, again pending EFS's discresion. Client shall not have 5 Source: PLAYBOY ENTERPRISES INC, 10-Q/A, 2/20/2009 input and approval rights regarding the hiring of key positions in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business, including replacement personnel. EFS agrees that ***** primary responsibilities will continue to be whatever EFS wants them to be.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 1.1 (j) clearly states EFS *agrees* to employ certain individuals from the Client by a specific date, with Client coordination, seniority carry-over, and Client input on key positions. The modified version introduces uncertainty by stating the hiring *may or may not* be complete, Client *shall not* coordinate, seniority *may or may not* carry over, and Client *shall not* have input on key positions, making several of the original provisions optional or denying them entirely. This is a direct in-text contradiction as the contract becomes ambiguous, making the parties rights and responsibilities very unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EFS agrees to employ certain individuals who are currently employed by the Client (names, titles and current salaries are listed in Exhibit 6), the hiring of whom may or may not be complete no later than March 1, 2008, pending EFS's discresion. Client shall not coordinate with EFS regarding the termination of these employees and their subsequent hire by EFS, as EFS will take care of that directly. Each of the hired employees shall be required to comply with all policies and procedures of EFS which are generally applicable to its employees; provided however that levels of seniority and tenure of hired employees (as they relate to vacation time, etc.) may or may not carry over to EFS, again pending EFS's discresion. Client shall not have 5 Source: PLAYBOY ENTERPRISES INC, 10-Q/A, 2/20/2009 input and approval rights regarding the hiring of key positions in connection with the Playboy Commerce Business, including replacement personnel. EFS agrees that ***** primary responsibilities will continue to be whatever EFS wants them to be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces significant uncertainties and optionality where the original text provided clear commitments. Specifically, the phrases "may or may not be complete", "Client shall not coordinate", "may or may not carry over", and "shall not have input" directly contradict the initial statements of agreement, coordination, and input detailed in the original text. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction, as the enforceability and obligations become unclear, creating a structural flaw as different sections of the modified document contain conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2. By EFS. EFS further represents and warrants that (a) the Websites and the Catalogs and all content contained therein (but specifically excluding the Playboy Marks and Playboy Content) do not and will not infringe any intellectual property right of any third party; (b) it has all necessary intellectual property rights in and to the Merchandise to be offered for sale through the Playboy Commerce Business; (c) it will at all times abide by and comply with the Privacy Policy and all other Laws applicable to operation of the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to the CANSPAM Act of 2003 and those related to privacy; (d) it (i) shall use its best efforts to ensure that all performers featured in any content and/or Merchandise offered for sale through the Catalogs and/or the Websites were at least eighteen (18) years of age at the time of production and (ii) shall comply with all record keeping, labeling and other requirements pursuant to Section 2257, as the same may be amended from time to time; (e) it will at all times take commercially reasonable steps to ensure the accuracy of all information contained on the Websites and in the Catalogs; and (f) the Playboy Commerce Business, including, without limitation, the Catalogs and Websites and all aspects thereof, will be operated in a workmanlike, timely and professional manner consistent with industry standards and EFS' operation of other third-party branded commerce offerings.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2. By EFS. EFS further represents and warrants that (a) the Websites and the Catalogs and all content contained therein (but specifically excluding the Playboy Marks and Playboy Content) may infringe on intellectual property rights of a third party; (b) it may or may not have all necessary intellectual property rights in and to the Merchandise to be offered for sale through the Playboy Commerce Business; (c) it may or may not abide by and comply with the Privacy Policy and all other Laws applicable to operation of the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to the CANSPAM Act of 2003 and those related to privacy; (d) it (i) does not need to use its best efforts to ensure that all performers featured in any content and/or Merchandise offered for sale through the Catalogs and/or the Websites were at least eighteen (18) years of age at the time of production and (ii) may or may not comply with all record keeping, labeling and other requirements pursuant to Section 2257, as the same may be amended from time to time; (e) it may not take steps to ensure the accuracy of all information contained on the Websites and in the Catalogs; and (f) the Playboy Commerce Business, including, without limitation, the Catalogs and Websites and all aspects thereof, will be operated in whatever manner deemed appropriate by EFS, inconsistent with industry standards or any other brand.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states EFS *warrants* several conditions: (a) no IP infringement, (b) all IP rights secured, (c) compliance with privacy and laws, (d) performers are of age, (e) accurate information, and (f) professional operation. The modified version introduces several direct contradictions: (a) *may* infringe IP, (b) *may or may not* have IP rights, (c) *may or may not* comply with laws, (d) no efforts needed to ensure age, (e) *may not* take steps for accuracy and (f) it will be operated in whatever way EFS seems appropriate, therefore this contradicting all the previous, as what used to be required is now allowed to not be achieved. These direct in-text contradictions create massive ambiguity, potentially invalidating the warranties and significantly shifting risk to Playboy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2. By EFS. EFS further represents and warrants that (a) the Websites and the Catalogs and all content contained therein (but specifically excluding the Playboy Marks and Playboy Content) may infringe on intellectual property rights of a third party; (b) it may or may not have all necessary intellectual property rights in and to the Merchandise to be offered for sale through the Playboy Commerce Business; (c) it may or may not abide by and comply with the Privacy Policy and all other Laws applicable to operation of the Playboy Commerce Business, including, but not limited to the CANSPAM Act of 2003 and those related to privacy; (d) it (i) does not need to use its best efforts to ensure that all performers featured in any content and/or Merchandise offered for sale through the Catalogs and/or the Websites were at least eighteen (18) years of age at the time of production and (ii) may or may not comply with all record keeping, labeling and other requirements pursuant to Section 2257, as the same may be amended from time to time; (e) it may not take steps to ensure the accuracy of all information contained on the Websites and in the Catalogs; and (f) the Playboy Commerce Business, including, without limitation, the Catalogs and Websites and all aspects thereof, will be operated in whatever manner deemed appropriate by EFS, inconsistent with industry standards or any other brand.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the warranties. The original text states that EFS 'represents and warrants' certain conditions, implying a guarantee. The changed text replaces these guarantees with possibilities ('may infringe', 'may or may not have', 'may not take steps'), directly conflicting with the concept of a warranty and creating a strong contradiction within the document by making what used to be a guaranteed state now uncertain. The Changed Text and Contradicted text are in the same location.
